message: Recovery can recapture a different read root for the same logical session when the original cwd path is a symlink.
evidence: recover reopens with s.cwd, openAt constructs newClientImpl(cwd), and canonicalReadRoot re-runs EvalSymlinks. If that cwd symlink is retargeted before recovery, later reads use a different captured root, violating the fixed-root contract.

message: ReadTextFile has a validation-to-open race that can allow a symlink escape after the path check.
evidence: resolveReadPath checks EvalSymlinks and returns a pathname, but os.ReadFile reopens that pathname later and follows symlinks. If an in-root file is swapped to an out-of-root symlink between those steps, the read is no longer root-scoped.

message: Recovery re-canonicalizes the original cwd instead of preserving the captured read root, so a symlink cwd can move the read boundary after a transparent session recovery.
evidence: recover reopens with s.tr.openAt(ctx, s.spec, s.cwd); openAt constructs newClientImpl(cwd), and newClientImpl calls canonicalReadRoot/EvalSymlinks. Because s.cwd stores the original cwd string, not the captured readRoot, retargeting a symlink cwd before recovery lets the new client capture a different root, violating the fixed-root acceptance criterion.

message: Root-scoped read tests bypass the ACP session wiring, so the transport-level capture of the opened session cwd is not tested.
evidence: The root-scoped ReadTextFile tests instantiate clientImpl directly with newClientAtRoot, while openAt's newClientImpl(cwd) path is not exercised. A regression that initializes the session client with the wrong valid root would still pass these tests.
